title: viola capillaris
intro: Viola capillaris, also known as Common Dog Violet, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the violet family. The medicinal properties of Viola capillaris are attributed to its leaves, flowers, and roots, which contain flavonoids, tannins, and saponins. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Viola capillaris can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and salves to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
